goal_1.goal_description: Establish Sheikha as the leading Islamic cloud provider with multi-cloud federation

goal_2.goal_description: Connect to banking, payment networks, and fintech to enable seamless transactions

goal_3.goal_description: Integrate with telecom, e-commerce, and retail to reach 500M+ users
goal_3.impact_area: Market Penetration, User Acquisition, Revenue Scaling

goal_4.goal_description: Secure approvals from regulators, Sharia councils, and government entities

goal_5.goal_description: Access cutting-edge AI models, LLMs, and compute infrastructure
goal_5.impact_area: Product Capability, AI Quality, Competitive Advantage

position_1.position_description: Central hub connecting technology partners, financial institutions, regulators, and users
position_1.requirement: Neutral, trustworthy, culturally aligned with all partners

position_2.position_description: Drive adoption of Sharia-compliant models, Islamic finance digitization, autonomous AI governance
position_2.requirement: Technical excellence, religious scholarship, regulatory expertise

position_3.position_description: Become the trusted alternative to secular big tech for Muslim-majority markets and businesses
position_3.requirement: Superior UX, ethical AI, transparent policies, religious legitimacy

activities: Partner identification based on strategic fit
phase_1.output: Due diligence report and executive briefing

activities: Negotiate key terms (revenue share, IP, data)

phase_4.output: Live integration, performance dashboard, SLA monitoring

phase_5.output: Quarterly reports, mutual growth initiatives, revenue insights

flow_1.security_level: Encrypted, Tokenized, Subject to consent agreements
flow_1.compliance: GDPR, Saudi DPA, Islamic data principles

flow_2.compliance: Banking regulation, Zakat compliance, Anti-money laundering

flow_3.compliance: API security standards, data residency rules

islamic_governance.authority_1: Quranic principles: Al-Amanah (Trust), At-Taraddi (Mutual Consent), Al-Adl (Justice)
islamic_governance.authority_2: Sunnah provisions: Honest dealings, avoiding Riba, Gharar, Ihtikaar (hoarding)
islamic_governance.authority_3: AAOIFI standards for Islamic financial institutions
islamic_governance.authority_4: Fatwa council review for new product features
islamic_governance.review_frequency: Quarterly or before major product launches

regulatory_compliance.compliance_1: Central Bank of Saudi Arabia (SAMA) approval for fintech integrations
regulatory_compliance.compliance_2: Ministry of Commerce approval for data handling practices
regulatory_compliance.compliance_4: OIC certification for Islamic product standards

encryption.in_transit: TLS 1.3+ for all data in flight
encryption.at_rest: AES-256 for sensitive data, FIPS 140-2 compliant
encryption.in_use: Homomorphic encryption for computation on encrypted data where required

information_security_framework.authentication: Multi-factor authentication (MFA), OAuth 2.0, OIDC, JWT tokens with short expiry
information_security_framework.access_control: Role-based access control (RBAC), Attribute-based access control (ABAC)

data_classification.public: No encryption needed, can be shared freely
data_classification.internal: Internal use only, encrypted, access logged
data_classification.confidential: Highly sensitive, end-to-end encrypted, audit trail mandatory
data_classification.restricted: Trade secrets, strategic, absolute access control

information_security_framework.monitoring: 24/7 SIEM monitoring, anomaly detection, real-time alerting

integration_patterns.description: RESTful APIs or GraphQL for data exchange and service calls
integration_patterns.use_case: Payment partners, fintech APIs, analytics platforms

integration_patterns.description: Webhooks, message queues, event streaming for real-time data
Batch file transfers (SFTP, S3) for periodic data exchange
integration_patterns.documentation: File format schema and transfer SLA

integration_patterns.description: Direct DB connections for real-time data sync (if allowed)
integration_patterns.use_case: Regulatory reporting systems, internal data warehouse
integration_patterns.documentation: Data sync agent configuration and monitoring

api_governance.deprecation_period: 12 months notice before deprecating API versions
api_governance.rate_limiting: Tiered by partner tier (Tier 1: 100k req/day, Tier 2: 50k, Tier 3: 10k)
api_governance.documentation: Auto-generated from code, maintained in developer portal

data_ownership.user_data: User owns their data. Sheikha processes on user's behalf. Partners can only access with explicit consent.
data_ownership.partner_data: Partner owns their data. Sheikha processes and protects on partner's behalf.
data_ownership.transactional_data: Shared ownership. Both parties have legal rights to use for agreed purposes.

data_governance.data_residency: Data must reside in Saudi Arabia or GCC region. No transfer to Western servers without explicit partner consent.

data_retention.user_data: Retained per user request. Deletion within 30 days of request.
data_retention.transaction_data: Retained 7 years for regulatory compliance, then archived.
data_retention.audit_logs: Retained minimum 3 years for compliance audits

data_sharing_rules.rule_1: Explicit consent required for any data sharing
data_sharing_rules.rule_2: Data minimization: Share only necessary fields
data_sharing_rules.rule_3: Purpose limitation: Data used only for agreed purposes
data_sharing_rules.rule_4: Pseudonymization: Personal data anonymized when possible

contract_5.contract_name: Revenue Share & Profit Distribution Agreement (Mudharabah Model)
key_sections: Profit/loss sharing formula (aligned with Islamic Mudharabah)
